details.text: Derivative: يُبَايِعُونَ means to pledge loyalty and allegiance. It comes from بَايَعَ which has origins in بَيْعٌ which means to sell. It is like saying: I am selling my life to you or for a cause.

Derivative: عَلَيْهُ (‘Alaihu) is normally عَلَيْهِ (Alaihi) in the Arabic language in the case where the ضَمير (Dameer/pronoun) ه refers to the ‘Ahd (oath made with Allah). The unique usage of عَلَيْهُ (‘Alaihu) contrary to the established norm has been explained to be due to the gravity attached to the ‘Ahd and as such it has been provided with uniqueness to demonstrate how heavy and serious the oath is.

- “Allah’s hand is above their hands” is an expression of great honour for the companions. In the process of giving a pledge, hands are place on top of hands to affirm the pledge. Here, Allah is stating that His hand is placed over their hands, as an affirmation for them and as an honouring for them.

Derivative: الْمُخَلَّفُونَ is from خَلَّفَ which means to leave behind. مُخَلَّف is the اِسْم المَفْعُول (Ism ul-Maf’ool) which means that it has been done to them i.e., الْمُخَلَّفُونَ means “those who were left behind” and not those who stayed behind. The difference is that the former implies that it was done to them whilst the latter implies that it was by their own doing.
Derivative: بُورًا means to descend and head towards ruin. It is used to refer to when your business stagnates until finally you are left in ruin.

Derivative: مَغَانِمَ means spoils of war. It comes from غَنَمٌ (Ghanam) which refers to wandering sheep with no obvious owner which are taken. غَنِيمَة is something that you find lying around where an owner is not present and you take it for yourself. The use of مَغَانِمَ here in the context of setting forth towards them, instead of saying towards battle or war, indicates to the ease of gaining victory and the spoils (i.e., of Khaibar).

Derivative: انْطَلَقْتُمْ from اِنْطَلَقَ. طَلاق and إِطْلاق both refer to a release. Likewise, اِنْطَلَقَ carries the same implication in the sense that it implies that after being held back you now set off and launch forward to accomplish your objective.
Derivative: أَثَابَ to cause to return back i.e., in recompense and repayment. To give ثوابٌ reward and recompense.
Derivative: ذَنْبِ in respect to the Messenger ﷺ does not refer to sins like it does with others but rather refers to strategic decisions and possible errors which none but Allah could be aware of.

the statements in this verse are connected to the first verse and the clear victory that allah mentioned. the sum of these statements is that you will no longer be impeded and the path towards victory will be laid out before.

- “Guide you towards or upon a straight path” can mean in terms of following the Deen and also in terms of being guided and facilitated towards fulfilling the objectives of the Deen and victory, in the case where the straight path refers to a path that has no crookedness in it or obstacles in the way.

Derivative: أَظْفَرَ comes from ظِفْر (Zhifr) which is a nail or claw. أَظْفَرَ (Azhfara) is used when a tiger catches its prey and grabs it with its claw or an eagle with its talon. It is used to mean to be given the upper hand over another like you were the claw that was going to catch them like the prey.

Derivative: تَزَيَّلُوا – زَالَ means for something to leave its place or to disappear. تَزَيَّلُوا from the meaning of زَيَّلَ means to be separated from i.e., where a part leaves another part and becomes distinguished from them.
Derivative: مَعَرَّةٌ refers to an embarrassing stigma, scandal, shame or dishonour.
Derivative: الْهَدْيَ refers to the animals that are marked for sacrifice during Hajj.
Derivative: مَعْكُوفًا from عَكِفَ which can mean to hold back, restrain and prevent.
- The addition of رِجَالٌ before مُؤْمِنُونَ and نِسَاءٌ before مُؤْمِنَاتٌ indicates that they were unknown to the Muslims.
Derivative: أَلْزَمَ means to make something or someone adhere, stick or commit to something i.e., to compel and impose.

Derivative: الْحَمِيَّةَ refers to the feelings or emotions attached to something that you are very protective of, have a lot of pride in and stubbornly hold on to with a fervour. حَمِيَّةَ الْجَاهِلِيَّةِ refers to the prideful and haughty fervour based on ignorance.
Derivative: يُظْهِرَ from أَظْهَرَ and with عَلَى it means to prevail, be victorious and manifest over.

- دِينِ الْحَقِّ means the Deen of truth and can also refer to the Deen being that which ensures that everyone receives their right (حَقٌّ) just as it can refer to purpose i.e., the Deen that has purpose.
Derivative: شَطْأَ is something that sprouts out from the edges of the crop – its offshoots.
Derivative: اسْتَغْلَظَ means to become thicker, firmer and tougher.
Derivative: آزَرَ which means to strengthen by one thing joining or entwining with another to give it strength and support.
Derivative: يَغِيظَ from غَاظَ which means to become enraged and riled up, or to be agitated and frustrated to the point of anger and exasperation.

- سِيمَا refers to a sign or mark. The phrase that “their sign will be on their faces” from the effect of Sujood (prostration)” does not refer to a specific physical mark being visible but rather another sign or mark that is special to them due to their worship of Allah in the case where the prostration represents the closest a person is to Allah in his worship.
details.arabic_part: سِيمَاهُمْ فِي وُجُوهِهِمْ مِنْ أَثَرِ السُّجُودِ

details.text: Derivative: عَزِيزًا means authoritative and indominable encompassing the meaning of might and honour i.e., a position of respect and esteem.
Derivative: تُعَزِّرُو from عَزَّرَ – تَعْزِير which means to aid someone out of respect that you have for them.
Derivative: تُوَقِّرُو from وَقَّرَ – تَوْقير and it means to hold in the highest level of respect and esteem due to the recognition of a person’s gravity and standing.
